skill.name: planning
skill.description: Transform rough feature ideas into actionable implementation plans with discovery questions, tradeoff analysis, task breakdowns, and plan storage guidance.
skill.summary: Create structured feature plans before implementation begins.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography are false positives from markdown fences, inline examples, and text substrings. No prompt injection, exfiltration, network access, or executable code was found. The skill has low filesystem risk because it declares read and write tools for creating plan documents.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at markdown code fences and inline examples. The file contains planning templates and one example git commit command, not executable code. Verdict: false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are markdown fences, inline path examples, or a sample git commit message. No instruction dynamically executes user input or invokes a shell outside documentation text.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are Text Substrings
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at text lines containing normal words such as description. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage appears in SKILL.md. Verdict: false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence lines contain metadata prose and a commit message example. There is no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection to evaluate.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access For Plan Documents
low_findings.description: The skill declares Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, and Write tools, and it recommends storing plans under docs/plans. This is expected for planning files but may modify workspace content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and storage path directly show intended filesystem use. The behavior is legitimate, but users should know the skill can create or edit planning documents.

content.user_title: Plan Feature Work With Structured Discovery
content.value_statement: Feature ideas often fail because requirements, constraints, and tasks are unclear. This skill turns a rough idea into a discovery summary and step-by-step implementation plan.
actual_capabilities: Guides requirement discovery with focused questions about goals, success measures, and constraints.
Asks for at least three implementation approaches and compares tradeoffs.
Produces a discovery summary with requirements, decisions, and open questions.
Structures implementation plans with goals, stack, dependencies, and affected files.
Breaks work into small tasks with tests, implementation notes, and commit messages.
Suggests storing plans under docs/plans with date-based filenames.
limitations: It does not implement the feature by itself.
Plan accuracy depends on accurate project context and user answers.
It may write planning files when used with write access.
It does not replace security, design, or architecture review for high-risk projects.

use_cases.title: Clarify a New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a vague feature request into requirements, constraints, open questions, and implementation options.

use_cases.title: Prepare Engineering Handoff
use_cases.description: Create a plan that a developer can follow without prior context about the codebase.

use_cases.title: Break Down TDD Work
use_cases.description: Divide implementation into small tasks that include testing, coding notes, and commit guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Small Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple implementation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning skill to help me plan this feature: [feature idea]. Ask discovery questions first.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Implementation Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning skill to compare at least three approaches for [feature]. Include constraints, tradeoffs, and a recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Developer Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning skill to write an implementation plan for [feature]. Assume the engineer has no prior codebase context.

prompt_templates.title: Build a TDD Task Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for task sequencing and quality gates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning skill to create a TDD-focused plan for [feature]. Include files, tests, task order, and commit messages.

output_examples.input: I need a plan for adding email login to a web app.
output: Discovery summary with goals, success measures, constraints, and open questions.
Three implementation approaches with tradeoffs and a recommended path.
A task list covering tests, affected files, implementation steps, and commits.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare a handoff for a developer who has never seen this project.
output: A clear implementation goal and technical context.
A file-by-file work plan with small tasks.
A checklist for requirements, tests, and completion criteria.

output_examples.input: Break down a risky refactor before coding starts.
output: Discovery questions about constraints, rollout, and compatibility.
Alternative refactor paths with risk notes.
A staged plan that separates test coverage, code changes, and validation.

best_practices: Answer discovery questions with concrete goals, constraints, and success measures.
Review the recommended approach before asking for the final implementation plan.
Store completed plans in a shared project location before execution begins.
anti_patterns: Skipping discovery and asking for tasks before requirements are clear.
Using the plan as a substitute for code review or security review.
Accepting generated file paths without checking the current repository structure.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces discovery summaries and implementation plans with tasks, files, tests, and commit guidance.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on planning before implementation.

faq.question: Can it ask follow-up questions?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses discovery questions to clarify goals, constraints, and decisions.

faq.question: Is it only for software teams?
faq.answer: It is mainly designed for feature development and engineering handoffs.

faq.question: Can it create files?
faq.answer: Yes, when the host tool grants write access, it can create or update planning documents.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
